signals.title: CHF/JPY — SNB Intervention + BoJ Tightening + Carry
signals.text: SNB Chairman Schlegel hints at near-term intervention readiness, stating medium-term inflation pressure is basically unchanged, while Rabobank highlights BoJ tightening and intervention risks for JPY. USD/JPY at 159.8, holding below 160.00, with a +292bp Fed-BoJ carry favoring USD. CHF/JPY at 202.544, with a -75bp carry premium favoring JPY, but SNB intervention risk could rapidly compress this further. JPY COT LF net short −86,249 (week ending 2026-05-26) vs BoJ hike bias (OIS), creating a crowded short-squeeze risk (USD/JPY downside if shorts cover) if BoJ acts. SNB's explicit intervention readiness introduces significant two-way volatility for JPY and CHF crosses. Watch for any official BoJ/SNB action or further verbal warnings.

signals.title: EUR/USD — ECB Hike Expectations + Volatility Premium
signals.text: Economists expect a June ECB rate hike as stagflation risk stays high (Reuters poll), with ECB policymaker Elderson noting prolonged war increases likelihood of second-round effects. EUR/USD at 1.1623. The options market shows an elevated vol premium with EUR/USD IV 7.1% vs HV30 3.9% (VRP +3.2%), indicating strong hedging demand. Despite hawkish headlines, EUR COT LF net short −26,311 (week ending 2026-05-26) suggests specs are not yet fully positioned for a hike. A confirmed ECB hike or hawkish forward guidance at the 11 Jun meeting is likely to trigger a short-squeeze.
evidence: Reuters poll: June ECB hike expected

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + G8 Laggard
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short −13,767 (week ending 2026-05-26) vs RBNZ hike bias (OIS) creates a short-squeeze risk if RBNZ delivers or signals a hike. NZD is also the weakest G8 currency this week, with a 1W avg of −0.461% (n=4 pairs), contributing to a 0.867% spread against the AUD leader. NZD/USD at 0.5900. The next RBNZ meeting (mkt: 20% hike priced) on 8 Jul is the key catalyst for this divergence.

signals.title: AUD/USD — G8 Leader + Carry Advantage + Negative CESI
signals.text: AUD is the strongest G8 currency this week, with a 1W avg of +0.405% (n=4 pairs), leading NZD by 0.867%. AUD/USD at 0.7172 benefits from a -70bp carry premium favoring AUD (AONIA 4.35% vs SOFR 3.65%). However, AUD CESI net −7 misses indicates data is consistently disappointing expectations, posing a headwind to sustained strength. Watch for RBA's next meeting on 16 Jun for any shift in stance that is likely to impact carry and sentiment.
